steps.annotation: Test condition: 85 >= 50 is TRUE - enter the block
Body runs because condition was true - prints pass

teachMe: 5 > 0 is true. The if-body runs and prints positive.
-3 > 0 is false. if-body skipped. Only end prints.
50 >= 100 is false. vip skipped, only bye prints.

clozeSentence: Conditional execution starts with the keyword ___
explanation: if is the keyword for conditional execution. It checks a condition before running its block.

clozeSentence: To test for greater-than, use the operator ___
explanation: > is the greater-than relational operator. score > 80 evaluates to true if score is bigger.

clozeSentence: After the condition, open the body block with ___
explanation: { opens the if-body. Statements between { and } only run when condition is true.

clozeSentence: The variable being tested is ___
explanation: The condition n > 0 references the variable n.

spec: Write an if statement (no body content) with condition score >= 50, opening brace included.
expectedAnswer: if (score >= 50) {
explanation: if (cond) { tests the condition, opens block.

spec: Write an if block: if balance >= 1000 print VIP customer.
explanation: if checks balance >= 1000. Body prints when condition is true.

spec: Write an if block: if temp > 100 print boiling.
explanation: Standard if-block pattern. Body runs when temp > 100.

spec: Write a complete program: declare int score = 75. If score >= 50 print pass.
explanation: Standard if pattern: condition true -> body executes.

spec: Write a complete program: read int age, if age >= 18 print adult.
explanation: Read input, then test with if and print accordingly.

question: Type the canonical if-block that prints positive when x > 0

steps.annotation: else block runs because if was false
else SKIPPED because if was true

teachMe: 7 > 10 is false. if skipped, else runs - prints small.
100 > 50 is true. if-body runs, else skipped. Prints high.
0 is false in if context. else runs - prints falsy.

clozeSentence: The keyword for the false branch is ___
explanation: else runs when the if condition was false.

clozeSentence: When a is not equal to b, print ___
explanation: else runs when a == b is false. The text inside the else body is what to fill.

clozeSentence: Since 5 > 10 is false, the ___ branch runs
explanation: When if condition is false, the else branch executes.

clozeSentence: Pair the if with ___ for the false case
explanation: if-else gives two exclusive paths. else handles when the condition fails.

spec: Write only the else clause: open brace, cout small, close brace.
explanation: else block prints small when paired if was false.

spec: Write an if/else block: if temp > 100 print hot else print cool.
explanation: Two exclusive branches based on temp > 100.

spec: Write an if/else block: if score >= 50 print pass else print fail.
explanation: Standard if/else for binary classification.

spec: Write a complete program: read int balance, if balance >= 0 print in credit else print overdrawn.
explanation: Read input, branch on sign with if/else.

spec: Write a complete program: read int age. If age >= 18 print adult else print minor.
explanation: if/else handles two age categories.

question: Type a canonical if/else: if x > 0 print pos else print neg

steps.annotation: Prints B - the else branch is skipped
Prints positive. The else if and else are SKIPPED

teachMe: 0 > 0 false. 0 < 0 false. else runs - prints zero.

clozeSentence: Chain another condition with ___ if
explanation: else if chains another test. Only runs when previous if was false.

clozeSentence: After else comes the keyword ___
explanation: else if is two keywords: else, then if with another condition.

clozeSentence: Chain conditions with ___ ___
explanation: else if chains - first matching branch wins.

clozeSentence: After all conditions fail, else runs the default ___
explanation: Final else is the catch-all when no else if matched.

spec: Write only the else if clause testing score >= 70 with body cout B.
explanation: else if pairs else with another conditional test.

spec: Write a 3-way chain for n: if n > 0 print pos else if n < 0 print neg else print zero.
explanation: Classic 3-way sign test using if/else if/else.

spec: Write grade chain: if score >= 90 A, else if >= 80 B, else C.
explanation: else if chain handles three score brackets.

spec: Write a complete program: read int score, classify as A (>=90), B (>=70), or C otherwise.
explanation: Complete classifier with read + 3-way chain.

spec: Write a complete program: read int hour, if hour < 12 print morning, else if hour < 18 print afternoon, else print evening.
explanation: Three-way classification of time-of-day.

question: Type a canonical 3-way if/else-if/else for n testing positive/negative/zero

steps.annotation: bool variable - holds true or false
Condition is the bool itself - if available is true, enter
stock > 0 is a bool expression - 0 > 0 is false. inStock = false
Test the bool - false skips body
code == 200 is a bool expression. 200 == 200 -> true
Body runs because expression evaluated to true

teachMe: ready is true. if(ready) means if(true) - body runs, prints go.
n=5. small = (5<10) = true. if(small) is true - prints yes.
a==b is 3==3 = true. if-body runs, prints equal.

code:
___ ok = (x > 0);
if (ok) cout << "yes";
clozeSentence: A variable holding true/false has type ___
explanation: bool is the C++ type for true/false values.

code:
bool flag = ___;
if (flag) cout << "on";
clozeSentence: The bool literal for the on-state is ___
explanation: true and false are the bool literals. true means the condition succeeds.

code: if (count == ___) cout << "empty";
clozeSentence: Test for zero count - the value is ___
explanation: count == 0 is a bool expression true when count equals zero.

code:
bool good = (score >= 50);
if (___) cout << "pass";
clozeSentence: Use the bool variable directly as the if condition: ___
explanation: if (good) reads the bool directly. No need to compare to true.

spec: Write a bool declaration that initializes a variable named valid to true.
expectedAnswer: bool valid = true;
explanation: bool type with literal true.

spec: Write code: declare bool inStock = (qty > 0). Then if inStock print available.
explanation: Comparison produces a bool. Use the bool var as condition.

spec: Write an if-block testing that sensor == 1: print active.
explanation: == produces bool. Use it directly in if condition.

spec: Write a complete program: read int qty, declare bool inStock = (qty > 0), if inStock print available else out.
explanation: Read input, build bool from comparison, branch on bool.

spec: Write a complete program: read int code, if code == 200 print OK else print Error.
explanation: == gives bool. Branch on equality test.

prompt: Write a bool variable named ok set to (count > 5).
expectedAnswer: bool ok = (count > 5);

variants.prompt: Write a bool variable named bigEnough set to (size >= 100).
Write a bool variable named matched set to (id == target).

prompt: Write a bool isAdult from age >= 18.
expectedAnswer: bool isAdult = (age >= 18);

question: Type the canonical bool-from-comparison: bool ok = (x > 0)
code: bool ok = (x > 0);

steps.annotation: Body inside braces - even one line
Always close with } - SIT102 grading needs braces
Close brace ends if-body. Lines after are unconditional
Close if, then open else. Brace per body

teachMe: Body runs, prints yes. Then code after braces runs unconditionally - prints end.
-1>0 false. if-body skipped. After } runs unconditionally - prints end.
Both lines inside { } run when condition is true. Prints a then b.

clozeSentence: Always open the if-body with ___ even for one line
explanation: Always wrap the body in {} - SIT102 expects this style.

clozeSentence: Close the if-block with ___
explanation: Every { needs a matching }.

clozeSentence: Open the else-body with ___
explanation: Always brace the else body too. Same rule as if.

clozeSentence: Close the if-body before else with ___
explanation: Each block needs its own pair of braces.

spec: Write a properly braced if-block: if x > 0 print pos, single statement body but with braces.
explanation: Always brace - even single-statement bodies.

spec: Write a properly braced if/else: if active print on else print off. Each body in its own braces.
explanation: Both bodies must be braced - even single line.

spec: Write a properly braced if-block: if items > 0 print Has items then print Stocked. Both lines inside the braces.
explanation: Multiple statements need braces; group both inside one { }.

spec: Write a complete program: read int n. If n > 0 print pos, else print not pos. Use braces on every body.
explanation: Both branches braced even for single statements.

spec: Write a complete program: read int stock. If stock > 0 print stocked twice (two cout calls inside one if-body).
explanation: Two-statement body must be braced.

prompt: Write an if-block with a 3-line body printing a, b, c when x > 0.

question: Type a fully-braced if-block where condition is x > 0 and body prints pos

Write an if-block testing balance < 0 that prints overdrawn.
